title: VerifyOtp
description: Action: `verify_otp`. Sent after the user receives an OTP code (delivered via a channel indicated in `otp_verification_required`) and enters it. The server validates the code and either resumes the agent workflow (success → `otp_verified` event + resumed stream) or rejects it (`otp_invalid` event with remaining attempt count). On max attempts exhausted the session is locked and a new `otp_sent` / `otp_verification_required` flow must be started.

Request.title: VerifyOtpRequest
Request.description: Fields sent by the client to submit an OTP code.

requestId.description: Must match the `requestId` from the `otp_verification_required` event being responded to.

sessionId.description: Session ID of the paused session.

code.description: The one-time password code entered by the user.

Response.title: VerifyOtpResponse
Response.description: No dedicated response event for `verify_otp`. On success the server emits `otp_verified` followed by the resumed stream. On failure it emits `otp_invalid`. This schema is provided for documentation completeness only.
No dedicated response — outcome signalled by `otp_verified` or `otp_invalid` events.
